Escape Plan (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $25,135,965Details
International Box Office $78,600,000
Worldwide Box Office $103,735,965
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,592,384
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,973,812
Total $15,566,196
Further financial details...

Synopsis

One of the world's foremost authorities on structural security agrees to take on one last job: breaking out of an ultra-secret, high-tech facility called "The Tomb.” Deceived and wrongly imprisoned, Ray Breslin (must recruit fellow inmate Emil Rottmayer to help devise a daring, nearly impossible plan to escape from the most protected and fortified prison ever built.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$9,885,732 (39.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:24.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$70,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,883 opening theaters/2,883 max. theaters, 3.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $36,976,242

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 18th, 2013 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: October 10th, 2013 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
October 10th, 2013 (Wide) (Malaysia)
October 10th, 2013 (Wide) (Lebanon)
October 10th, 2013 (Wide) (Jordan)
October 11th, 2013 (Wide) (Brazil)
Video Release: February 4th, 2014 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for violence and language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2285, 8/14/2013)
Running Time: 115 minutes
Franchise: Escape Plan
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animated Sequences, Digital Cinematography, Intertitle, Voiceover/Narration

Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Summit Entertainment, Emmett/Furla Films, Mark Canton, Envision Entertainment, Boies/Schiller
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Arabic, German

Synopsis

One of the world's foremost authorities on structural security agrees to take on one last job: breaking out of an ultra-secret, high-tech facility called "The Tomb.” Deceived and wrongly imprisoned, Ray Breslin (must recruit fellow inmate Emil Rottmayer to help devise a daring, nearly impossible plan to escape from the most protected and fortified prison ever built.
